patch.value: workflows.json maps global role slots but has no per-type state-machine; a project cannot enforce that Story must pass through in_review before closed. Biggest remaining domain-governance gap.
type_workflows: [{type, allowed_transitions:[[from,to]...]}] validated on pm update --status with warn|strict per governance policy; tests; CONFIGURATION.md.
workflows.json maps global role slots but has no per-type state-machine; a project cannot enforce that Story must pass through in_review before closed. Biggest remaining domain-governance gap.

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: Implemented per-type allowed-transitions. SHAPE: schema/workflows.json extended to {workflow:{...}, type_workflows:[{type, allowed_transitions:[[from,to],...]}]}. Loader readWorkflowFromSchemaFile unchanged; added readTypeWorkflowsFromSchemaFile and threaded loadedTypeWorkflows through loadRuntimeSchemaFromOptionalFiles into merged RuntimeSchemaSettings. Added type_workflows? to RuntimeSchemaSettings (types.ts) + settings-validator (array of {type:string, allowed_transitions: [string,string][]} via a tuple-of-2-strings Check) + normalizeTypeWorkflowDefinitions in runtime-schema.ts (keeps raw trimmed type, lowercases for dedup/compare, normalizes from/to via normalizeStatusToken, drops empty/malformed). ENFORCEMENT MODE: new governance.workflow_enforcement (off|warn|strict, DEFAULT off so existing projects unaffected), read RAW from settings.governance.workflow_enforcement (NOT via resolveGovernanceKnobs preset path), carried through persist same as create_default_type. Settable via config set governance-workflow-enforcement. PURE HELPER src/core/schema/type-workflows.ts (resolveTypeWorkflows + evaluateTransition + describeAllowedTransitions) is dependency-light, in vitest include, 100% covered. Semantics: type with no entry = unrestricted; with entry = only listed [from,to] (resolved via statusRegistry.alias_to_id, case-insensitive); same from==to no-op always allowed. INJECTION POINT: runUpdate reads item status+type once BEFORE the close-reroute (only when enforcement!=off AND type_workflows present AND --status set, so off-path is zero-cost), so transitions toward close are gated too; strict throws PmCliError(EXIT_CODE.USAGE) with allowed-transitions hint; warn appends 'workflow_transition_not_allowed: ...' to result.warnings (threaded into BOTH the reroute return and the mutate return). Per-type lookup uses item's CURRENT document.metadata.type. Smoke-tested strict (disallow open->blocked, allow open->in_progress, gated open->closed before reroute), warn (applies+warns), off (ignores), unrestricted Task unaffected. Tests in status-normalization.spec.ts (helper), settings-validator.spec.ts (validation), update-command.spec.ts (enforcement); docs in CONFIGURATION.md (Per-Type Workflows section) + COMMANDS.md.

patch.value: Added per-type workflow allowed-transitions: schema/workflows.json gains type_workflows:[{type, allowed_transitions:[[from,to]]}]; new governance.workflow_enforcement (off|warn|strict, default off) settable via pm config set. New pure helper type-workflows.ts (resolveTypeWorkflows/evaluateTransition/describeAllowedTransitions), 100% covered. Enforced in runUpdate before the close-reroute (so close transitions are gated too); strict throws, warn surfaces workflow_transition_not_allowed, off is a no-op. Type with no rule is unrestricted; self-transition always allowed; statuses resolved via alias_to_id.
pm update --status validates transitions per type_workflows under warn|strict; default off keeps existing behavior unchanged.

Verified in temp dir: strict rejects open->blocked + open->closed (gated before reroute), allows open->in_progress; warn surfaces the warning and applies; off/unconfigured types unaffected. Full coverage gate green (2195 tests, 100%).

Delivered per-type workflow allowed-transitions (schema/workflows.json type_workflows + governance.workflow_enforcement off|warn|strict, default off), enforced in pm update --status before the close-reroute. New type-workflows.ts pure helper 100% covered; settings-validator + docs/CONFIGURATION.md + tests. Default-off keeps existing projects unchanged. Verified in temp dir. Part of schema-customization-completion PR (plan pm-z5nb).

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: PR #106 review remediation: an explicit empty allowed_transitions array is now correctly treated as a DENY-ALL rule (only same-status no-ops allowed) instead of being dropped as no-rule (which made the type unrestricted). Fixed in BOTH normalization layers: resolveTypeWorkflows (type-workflows.ts) AND normalizeTypeWorkflowDefinitions (runtime-schema.ts) -- the latter was the real bug, since settings.schema is normalized before resolveTypeWorkflows ever runs, so without it the empty rule was stripped at file-load and enforcement never engaged. Both now keep entries with a valid type and an array allowed_transitions (even empty); only missing/empty type or non-array transitions are dropped. Also added defensive guards: resolveStatusId optional-chains the alias map, and update.ts workflow-enforcement reads default metadata type/status/toStatus to empty strings. Verified end-to-end via update-command.spec.ts deny-all tests (strict denies cross-status, allows no-op) and a live smoke run.

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: Review round-2 (Codex) hardening: per-type workflow enforcement now gates on the EFFECTIVE (post-update) type. When a single pm update changes both --type and --status, the transition is evaluated against the resolved target type (resolveTypeName(options.type)) instead of the item's current type — so a combined --type/--status change can't bypass the target type's rule, nor be wrongly blocked by the pre-update type's rule. Verified both directions in temp-dir smoke; full coverage gate 100%.

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: Review round-3 (Codex) hardening: (C8) a NONEMPTY allowed_transitions whose pairs are all malformed (e.g. a 3-element tuple typo) is now DROPPED (type stays unrestricted) instead of collapsing to an accidental deny-all under strict enforcement. Only an EXPLICIT empty [] remains a deliberate deny-all. Fixed consistently in both normalization layers (resolveTypeWorkflows + normalizeTypeWorkflowDefinitions) via a denyAll flag that is only set when the raw array is literally empty. Tests added for the malformed-drop case. NOTE: Codex's separate C7 finding (file-backed type_workflows/statuses/types leaking into settings.json on writeSettings) is a PRE-EXISTING cross-cutting bug filed as [[pm-haak]] for a holistic fix — out of scope for this feature PR.
